report_name: 2026 World Cup Faces Unprecedented Cyber Threats from Nations and Criminals

threat_actors.name: Handala Hack Team
threat_actors.description: An Iran-nexus threat actor with a history of targeting U.S. critical infrastructure with destructive wiper malware. They are considered a primary concern for the 2026 World Cup host cities' municipal services.
threat_actors.goals: Disruption of critical infrastructure and political messaging.

threat_actors.name: CyberAv3ngers
threat_actors.description: An Iran-nexus threat actor specializing in targeting industrial control systems (ICS) in the U.S., including water and energy systems. They are expected to leverage known vulnerabilities and stolen credentials to disrupt municipal services during the 2026 World Cup.
threat_actors.goals: Disruption of critical infrastructure, particularly water, wastewater, and power grids.

threat_actors.name: NoName057(16)
threat_actors.description: A pro-Russian hacktivist group expected to conduct widespread DDoS attacks and information operations against the 2026 World Cup. Their goal is to disrupt services, sow chaos, and advance pro-Kremlin narratives.
threat_actors.goals: Disruption of services, spreading chaos, and advancing political narratives.

threat_actors.name: Financially Motivated Cybercriminals
threat_actors.description: A general category of threat actors expected to conduct high-volume, low-complexity attacks targeting the general public attending the 2026 World Cup. Their methods include phishing, ticket fraud, and malicious QR codes.
threat_actors.goals: Financial gain through fraud and theft.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used by financially motivated criminals with lure themes including fake ticket lotteries, accommodation deals, and official-looking communications to target the general public during the 2026 World Cup.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Attachment
attack_patterns.description: Malicious QR codes will be used to direct victims to credential harvesting sites or to install malware, presented as a form of spearphishing attachment in the context of the 2026 World Cup threats.

attack_patterns.name: Acquire Infrastructure: Domains
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors are expected to register typosquatted domains mimicking FIFA, host city, and sponsor websites to conduct phishing and fraud campaigns against 2026 World Cup spectators.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: State-aligned groups like CyberAv3ngers are expected to use stolen credentials to gain access to and disrupt ICS/SCADA systems in host cities for the 2026 World Cup.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: Iran-nexus groups like Handala Hack Team are expected to use wiper malware to render systems inoperable, causing maximum disruption to tournament logistics or municipal services.

attack_patterns.name: Network Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: Pro-Russian hacktivist groups like NoName057(16) are expected to use botnets to overwhelm the websites of organizers, sponsors, and public services related to the 2026 World Cup.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: Execution of encoded PowerShell payloads is a common pattern for fileless malware execution, suggested as a hunting hint for security teams monitoring for threats against the 2026 World Cup.

identities.name: 2026 FIFA World Cup
identities.description: The international football tournament hosted across the US, Canada, and Mexico, identified as a prime target for a wide range of cyber threats.

identities.name: Paris 2024 Olympics
identities.description: A past major sporting event cited as a case study, having faced over 140 cyber events, highlighting the risks for the 2026 World Cup.

identities.name: CISA
identities.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency, which provided an assessment on the vulnerability of U.S. water utilities, a key infrastructure component for the World Cup.

identities.name: U.S. water utilities
identities.description: A class of critical infrastructure identified as highly vulnerable and a prime target for state-aligned actors during the 2026 World Cup.

infrastructure.name: Typosquatted Domains for World Cup
infrastructure.description: Fraudulent domains mimicking FIFA, host cities, and sponsors to be used for phishing and credential harvesting.

infrastructure.name: DDoS Botnets
infrastructure.description: Botnets expected to be used by pro-Russian hacktivists to conduct Network Denial of Service attacks against World Cup-related websites and services.

indicators.name: Potential 2026 World Cup Phishing URL
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential 2026 World Cup Phishing URL
id: 2a9b3c1d-e8f0-4a5b-8c7d-6e5f4a3b2c1d
status: experimental
description: 'Detects URLs containing keywords related to 2026 World Cup tickets, which could indicate phishing or fraud attempts. This is based on a hunting hint from a Unit 42 report.'
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/fifa-world-cup-attack-surface/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-28
tags:
  - attack.t1566
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains|all:
      - 'worldcup'
      - '2026'
      - 'ticket'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects URLs containing keywords related to 2026 World Cup tickets, which could indicate phishing or fraud attempts.

indicators.name: Potential Malicious World Cup Android App
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Malicious World Cup Android App
id: 8b7c6d5e-4f3a-4b2c-9d1e-0f9e8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the presence of a file named ''WorldCup_Schedule.apk'', which could be an unofficial and malicious application. This is based on a hunting hint from a Unit 42 report.'
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/fifa-world-cup-attack-surface/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-28
tags:
  - attack.t1204.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\WorldCup_Schedule.apk'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of a file named 'WorldCup_Schedule.apk', which could be an unofficial and malicious application targeting World Cup fans.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Payload Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Encoded Payload Execution
id: 1d2e3f4a-5b6c-4d7e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: 'Detects the execution of PowerShell with an encoded command, a common technique for fileless malware execution. This is based on a hunting hint from a Unit 42 report.'
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/fifa-world-cup-attack-surface/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-28
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains: ' -enc '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PowerShell with an encoded command, a common technique for fileless malware execution.

relationships.source_name: Potential Malicious World Cup Android App
